key_themes: Solid revenue and organic growth with strategic pivot to electronics, software, AI-enabled security
Q4 2025 profit miss and cautious 2026 guidance (residential weakness) triggered ~8-9% post-earnings sell-off
Capital returns via dividend increase and $500M buyback expansion; analyst consensus PT implies ~18% upside despite some PT cuts
Mixed analyst actions (new Buy initiation offset by several target reductions); Hold consensus overall
sources_checked: Company investor releases (earnings, dividend, buyback, Q1 webcast)
MarketBeat analyst consensus and recent actions
Yahoo Finance / Reuters news and estimates
TipRanks / financial media on ratings and strategy
Recent web coverage on residential demand and electronics push
summary: Allegion delivered revenue growth in FY2025 and is executing a multi-year shift toward higher-margin electronic/software/AI products while raising its dividend and expanding buybacks, supporting moderate optimism and meaningful analyst price-target upside from current levels near $144. However, a Q4 profit miss on weak U.S. residential demand plus 2026 guidance that disappointed investors caused a sharp sell-off in February, with several brokerages trimming targets in early 2026. The biggest risk is prolonged residential market softness and execution on the electronics transition ahead of Q1 results on April 28.

recent_posts.highlight: Allegion expands share repurchase authorization by $500M alongside quarterly dividend declaration
Initiates coverage with Buy/Outperform rating, highlighting long-term electronics and software opportunity
Consensus Hold with $171 PT (18%+ upside) but recent PT cuts by Barclays, Wells Fargo, Goldman, and JPM
Q4 profit miss on weak residential demand; 2026 EPS guidance midpoint below expectations, stock drops sharply
